on an equal basis with others:
a. Enjoy the right to liberty and security of person;
b. Are not deprived of their liberty unlawfully or arbitrarily, 
and that any deprivation of liberty is in conformity with the 
law, and that the existence of a disability shall in no case 
justify a deprivation of liberty.
ARTICLE 21 – FREEDOM OF EXPRESSION AND OPINION, AND 
ACCESS TO INFORMATION
States Parties shall take all appropriate measures to 
ensure that persons with disabilities can exercise the 
right to freedom of expression and opinion, including the 
freedom to seek, receive and impart information and ideas 
on an equal basis with others and through all forms of 
communication of their choice.
[2025] 4 S.C.R.  
271
In Re: Recruitment of Visually Impaired in Judicial Services
ARTICLE 24 – EDUCATION
States Parties recognize the right of persons with 
disabilities to education. With a view to realizing this right 
without discrimination and on the basis of equal opportunity,
